treatment_information.dosage_or_details: 5 mg haloperidol, 2 mg Ativan; additional 5 mg haloperidol, 2 mg Ativan; or IM and IV administration of chlorpromazine, lorazepam, diphenhydramine, ketamine
treatment_information.treatment_response: Patient was generally compliant with home medications; exhibited agitation and aggression during ED course.

treatment_timeline.event: Patient presented to group home with agitation and was brought to ED by EMS.
Initial ED consult indicated significant agitation and the need for psychiatric evaluation.
Received 5 mg haloperidol and 2 mg Ativan upon arrival due to agitation.
Received additional doses of 5 mg haloperidol and 2 mg Ativan for continued agitation.
Hospitalization was deemed appropriate due to acute decline from baseline status.
Discharge medications prescribed including diazepam, omeprazole, propranolol, chlorpromazine, and lurasidone.

patient_outcome.final_status: discharged in stable condition with improved mental status
patient_outcome.follow_up_details: Follow up with all outpatient appointments as listed.
patient_outcome.long_term_prognosis: Expected to continue outpatient care and medication as directed.
patient_outcome.quality_of_life: The patient showed improvement in mood and was able to engage appropriately at discharge.
patient_outcome.patient_perspective: Patient expressed concerns about their treatment and institutionalization, indicating a struggle with mental health instability.
